mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the risk-free discount rate applied to equity valuations, generating positive forward returns for the S&P 500 over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or forward 20-day returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.5pp, or the signal loses predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, which drives corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform single-series baselines, or forward 60-day returns are negative in over 60% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during periods of low inflation reflects healthy capacity utilization without pricing pressure, supporting equity valuations and generating positive forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses or becomes statistically insignificant when CPIAUCSL exceeds 0.3% monthly growth, or Rank IC drops below 0.01 in the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP growth rates signal strengthening economic activity that immediately boosts market sentiment and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns show zero or negative correlation with GDPC1 growth rates across multiple rolling windows, or t-statistic remains below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level exceeding 2.0% triggers a monetary tightening regime that compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 50% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0%, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates indicates labor market tightening that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses statistical significance or forward returns turn negative when PAYEMS and UNRATE move in opposite directions.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during periods of elevated unemployment signals a deepening economic contraction that rapidly erodes corporate profit expectations and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over one week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in over 55% of observations when INDPRO declines alongside UNRATE above 8.0%, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark.
